---
title: "Revealjs Progress Bar"
subtitle: "A section-aware progress bar for Quarto presentations"
author: "Alexandre Parent"
format:
  revealjs:
    center: true
    progress: false
    theme: default
    css: custom.css
    progress-bar:
      animate-overview-exit: true
      section-widths: proportional
      show-slide-numbers: true
      counted-slides: all
filters:
  - revealjs-progress-bar
---

## Why This Extension?

`revealjs-progress-bar` adds a section-aware progress bar to Quarto RevealJS presentations.

It is designed for decks where the audience should know where they are in the story, not just how many slides remain.

## Presentation plan {.progress-overview}

# Features {data-progress-label="Features"}

## Features overview

- One segment per section
- Slide ticks inside each section
- Adaptive slide numbers on hover
- Clickable navigation

## Navigation

Hover over the progress bar to reveal slide numbers. Dense decks automatically use a global sampling step so labels stay readable.

Click a section label to jump to the beginning of a section.

Click inside a section track to jump to a specific slide in that section.

## Fragments

Fragments do not count as progress.

. . .

This fragment appears without advancing the progress bar.

. . .

So does this one.

# Vertical Sections {data-progress-label="Vertical"}

## Title Slides Are Ignored

This section started with a title slide.

That title slide establishes the section, but it does not count as progress.

## First Content Slide

The bar starts advancing on the first content slide in the section.

## Second Content Slide

The tick marks show how many content slides are in this section.

# Customization {data-progress-label="Style"}

## CSS Variables

You can customize the visual style using CSS variables.

```css
:root {
  --rpb-fill-color: #2563eb;
  --rpb-track-color: rgba(37, 99, 235, 0.18);
  --rpb-label-active-color: #111827;
}
```

## YAML Options

```yaml
format:
  revealjs:
    progress: false
    progress-bar:
      animate-overview-exit: true
      section-widths: equal
      show-slide-numbers: true
      counted-slides: all
filters:
  - revealjs-progress-bar
```

```yaml
progress-bar:
  animate-overview-exit: true
  section-widths: equal
  show-slide-numbers: true
  counted-slides: all
```

Use `section-widths: proportional` when each slide should occupy the same width in the bar.

Set `show-slide-numbers: false` to hide numbers on the bar. Use `counted-slides: main` to number only progress content slides instead of matching Quarto's full slide numbering.

## Hide On One Slide {.hide-progress-bar}

Add `.hide-progress-bar` to a slide heading to hide the progress bar on that slide only.

This slide is still part of the progress calculation. Only the bar display is hidden, and the bar appears again automatically on the next regular progress slide.

Use this for appendix slides, transition slides, or moments where the slide needs a cleaner stage:

```markdown
## Appendix Detail {.hide-progress-bar}
```

## Section Labels

Use `data-progress-label` for short labels in the progress bar.

```markdown
# Long Section Title {data-progress-label="Short"}
```

# Finish {data-progress-label=""}

## Install

```bash
quarto add alpa12/revealjs-progress-bar
```

## Thank You

The deck is complete.
